name: KrillApp.DataPoint.Filter.Deadband
title: Deadband
description: Rejects DataPoint snapshots where the change from the previous value is smaller than a configured threshold. Computes |new_value - previous_value| and discards the snapshot if the change is below the deadband value, which is stored in this filter's own meta.snapshot.value as a stringified number. Suppresses noise from sensors by requiring a minimum change magnitude between consecutive readings. The filter only takes effect when the owning DataPoint lists it in its meta.inputs — parenting alone does nothing.
shortDescription: Discards snapshots where the value change from previous is smaller than a threshold.
llmPurpose: Suppresses sensor noise by rejecting snapshots where the value change from the previous reading is below a configured minimum magnitude.
llmBehavior: compares |new_value - previous_value| against the threshold in its own meta.snapshot.value
discards snapshot if change is too small
llmInputs: deadband threshold stored in this filter's meta.snapshot.value (stringified number)
llmPromptHints: only record if value changes by at least
llmCreationHints: Create a Deadband filter when the user wants to suppress small fluctuations in sensor data. Store the minimum change magnitude as a stringified number in the filter's meta.snapshot.value (FilterMetaData has no value field), and list the filter in the owning DataPoint's meta.inputs — a filter merely parented under a DataPoint does nothing.

llmConnectionHints.role: leaf filter node that gates incoming snapshot values based on change magnitude
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Passive: this filter uses no sources or invocationTriggers; the owning DataPoint must list it in its meta.inputs for it to be evaluated during ingest, and the threshold lives in the filter's own meta.snapshot.value.

llmExamples: Only record temperature changes of at least 0.5 degrees.
Ignore pressure changes smaller than 1 PSI.
Suppress sensor noise — only log if value changes by 2 or more.
